summary: Gruber annotates a photo of his workspace submitted to a 'Workspaces' project, walking through each item on and around his desk. He catalogs his Ikea furniture, aging Macs, peripherals, and bookshelf with characteristic dry humor. The post is a lighthearted personal inventory that reveals his setup as a Mac-centric developer and writer in 2002.
key_points: Gruber's workspace centers on two Macs: an older PowerMac 9600 running OS 9 and a PowerBook G3 running OS X 10.2
The post showcases his identity as a Mac power user, regex enthusiast, and O'Reilly book collector
Self-deprecating humor pervades the descriptions, from the Ikea desk name to the worn Yankees cap
money_quotes: Solidly constructed, highly configurable, but saddled with the unfortunate product name of "Jerker".
192 MB of RAM, an amount which was once considered a handsome sum of memory, but which can now be obtained for free in a box of Frosted Flakes.
Genuine New York Yankees baseball cap, size 7 and one-quarter, circa 1990; it is a bit, um, well-worn.
key_insight: A playful, self-aware tour of a 2002 Mac enthusiast's desk that reveals personality through the details of everyday objects.
